notes: Spec asserts state stays ready during two synchronous slash commands (/help, /status). Vibe never writes a slash command to messages.jsonl: _handle_command short-circuits before the input joins the agent-loop message list, and only that list is persisted (session_logger.persist_messages). The daemon tails messages.jsonl, so a slash command yields no ParsedEvent and no state transition — no local-command filter is needed (unlike claudecode). A warm-up turn goes FIRST to materialize the transcript (vibe writes messages.jsonl only after a real LLM turn) and birth an observable session; after warm_turn_end returns to ready, slash_window pins ready with duration_at_least_ms:4000 and invariants forbidding transitions to working/waiting — those are what would fire if /help or /status (incorrectly) drove a model call. Driver: wait_turn is a stubbed SEAM — record ports it (driver-gap) before recording; the slash step is fully supported. BACKFLOW (live recording): mistral-vibe is a presession adapter — a transient proc-<PID> row is born ready before the real session_<id> transcript dir appears, then reconciles to a DIFFERENT session_id, so the birth is modeled as two phases (presession_birth → session_birth new_session) templated from the codex sibling, with every post-birth phase anchored same_session_as session_birth. OBSERVATIONS reduced to agent-only (cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false): the offline replay golden stages only transcript.jsonl, so vibe's meta.json sidecar model + tokens are unverifiable offline (same as kiro-cli). KNOWN_FAILING (GitHub issue #905): the slash_window phase FAILS on the committed recording (verifies 5/6). The daemon's FilesUnderRoot tail treats a CONTENT-LESS touch of messages.jsonl (mtime bump with no new appended line) as activity and force-works the ready session, producing a spurious ready->working->ready blip during the /help + /status window even though no slash command is ever written to the transcript. The spec asserts the CORRECT behavior (state stays ready; no ->working/->waiting during the slash window) on purpose; known_failing records that the daemon currently violates it. When the daemon ignores content-less touches (mtime-only, no new line) and stops force-working on them, drop this flag.
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Synchronous slash command (no LLM call)

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)
Real session_<id> reconciles (different session_id) when the transcript directory appears, born ready
vibe PID bound to the real session (matched by cwd)
Warm-up prompt drives ready -> working (real LLM turn; materializes messages.jsonl so later slash steps have a settled session to measure against)
Warm-up turn completes back to ready
Spec: state stays ready throughout /help and /status — neither synchronous slash command is written to messages.jsonl, so no transition fires
